EnglishWord.title: EnglishWord
EnglishWord.description: Represents an English word and its associated data for learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the English word entry.

mongolianMeaning.description: The Mongolian translation/meaning of the English word.

memorized.description: Indicates whether the user has memorized the word.

EnglishGrammarRule.title: EnglishGrammarRule
EnglishGrammarRule.description: Represents a grammar rule and its explanation for English language learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the grammar rule entry.

ruleName.description: The name of the grammar rule.

explanation.description: A detailed explanation of the grammar rule.

exampleSentence.description: An example sentence demonstrating the usage of the grammar rule.

JapaneseCharacter.title: JapaneseCharacter
JapaneseCharacter.description: Represents a Hiragana or Katakana character for Japanese language learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Japanese character entry.

character.description: The Japanese character itself (Hiragana or Katakana).

memorized.description: Indicates whether the user has memorized the character.

type.description: The type of character, either Hiragana or Katakana.

JapaneseWord.title: JapaneseWord
JapaneseWord.description: Represents a Japanese word and its associated data for learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Japanese word entry.

romaji.description: The Romaji representation of the Japanese word.

mongolianMeaning.description: The Mongolian translation/meaning of the Japanese word.

memorized.description: Indicates whether the user has memorized the word.

JapaneseGrammarRule.title: JapaneseGrammarRule
JapaneseGrammarRule.description: Represents a grammar rule and its explanation for Japanese language learning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the grammar rule entry.

ruleName.description: The name of the grammar rule (e.g., JLPT N5).

explanation.description: A detailed explanation of the grammar rule.

exampleSentence.description: An example sentence demonstrating the usage of the grammar rule.

ProgrammingConcept.title: ProgrammingConcept
ProgrammingConcept.description: Represents a programming concept (e.g., JavaScript, React) and its explanation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the programming concept entry.

topic.description: The name of the programming concept (e.g., JavaScript).

explanation.description: A short explanation of the programming concept.

CodeSnippet.title: CodeSnippet
CodeSnippet.description: Represents a code snippet or syntax example for programming.

id.description: Unique identifier for the code snippet entry.

title.description: A descriptive title for the code snippet.

language.description: The programming language of the code snippet (e.g., JavaScript, HTML).

ProgrammingProgress.title: ProgrammingProgress
ProgrammingProgress.description: Represents the user's progress in learning a programming concept.

id.description: Unique identifier for the programming progress entry.

learned.description: Indicates whether the user has learned the concept.

practicing.description: Indicates whether the user is currently practicing the concept.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project in the user's portfolio.

description.description: A brief description of the project.

imageUrl.description: URL of the image for the project.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a skill or area of expertise.

title.description: The name or title of the skill (e.g., React, UI/UX Design).

description.description: A description of the skill and level of expertise.

definition.description: Stores English words for a specific user. Path-based ownership is enforced via the `userId` parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the English word.

params.name: englishWordId
params.description: The ID of the English word.

params.description: The ID of the English grammar rule.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store data related to English, Japanese, and Programming concepts, along with user progress and portfolio projects. User data is implicitly managed through Firebase Authentication. To adhere to Authorization Independence, no `get()` calls are necessary for security rules. This is achieved by structuring data in a way that authorization can be determined directly from the request and document data.

*   **User-Owned Data:** English words, English grammar rules, Japanese characters, Japanese words, Japanese grammar rules, Programming concepts, Code snippets, Programming progress, Skills, and Projects are all implicitly owned by the authenticated user.

*   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  Each top-level collection segregates data with distinct security postures.  The structure avoids mixing data requiring different access controls within the same collection. List operations are secure, as rules only need to check `request.auth.uid` against the owner ID in the path.
